title: Fitchburg fire chief on paid leave pending external investigation, mayor says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Fitchburg Fire Chief Dante Suarez is on paid leave pending an external investigation, confirmed by Mayor Sam Squailia.
Assistant Chief Patrick Haverty has been appointed acting chief of the Fitchburg Fire Department.
Suarez's leave started on February 26; further details are withheld due to personnel matters.

title: Person seriously injured in Seaport stabbing
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man was seriously injured with stab wounds to the abdomen and leg outside 72 Seaport Blvd early Sunday.
Emergency services transported the victim to a local hospital; his current condition is unknown, according to Boston Police.
No suspects have been identified and no arrests have been made; the investigation is ongoing, police stated.

title: Here’s what happened during the first day of Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons's trial began Monday in Essex County Superior Court, where she opted for a bench trial.
Fitzsimmons, a North Andover officer, is charged with assault and battery; she claims she attempted suicide when shot.
Prosecution argues Fitzsimmons aimed a gun at Officer Patrick Noonan, prompting him to shoot; defense disputes this claim.

title: Bruins host Blackstone Valley co-op hockey team at practice after run to R.I. state title ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins hosted the Blackstone Valley co-op hockey team at practice following their Rhode Island Division II state title win.
Blackstone Valley's Colin Dorgan scored crucial goals to lead his team to victory after a family tragedy during the season.
Bruins players, including Jeremy Swayman, interacted with the high school team, signing gear and taking photos at Warrior Ice Arena.

title: Patriots sign offensive tackle James Hudson
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots signed offensive tackle James Hudson, who has 60 career games with 19 starts in the NFL.
Hudson, a 6-foot-5, 313-pound lineman, was released by the Giants this month and previously played for the Browns.
He'll provide depth, primarily at right tackle, backing up Morgan Moses, who started all 17 games last season.

title: As waiting game continues with James Hagens, Bruins coach has no issue adding prospects to lineup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins prospect James Hagens remains unsigned after ending his sophomore season at Boston College.
Hagens, a 2025 top-10 NHL draft pick, recorded 47 points in 34 games for Boston College this season.
Bruins coach Marco Sturm is open to adding young players if they are ready, amid ongoing talks with Hagens.

title: ESPN reporter explains why June 1st could be a big day for teams looking to trade for A.J. Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: ESPN’s Mike Reiss notes June 1st could significantly reduce the Eagles' dead money if they trade A.J. Brown.
Trading Brown after June 1st reduces Eagles' dead cap from $43.5 million to $16.353 million, impacting trade timing.
The Patriots remain interested in acquiring Brown, with potential deals involving 2026 first-round picks being speculated.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her mother’s fight against cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Allyson Bogdanski, 45, from Medford, runs the Boston Marathon to honor her mother's battle against breast cancer.
Bogdanski's mother, a patient at Dana-Farber, survived breast cancer twice with their care and support.
Bogdanski dedicates each mile to her mother's strength and the work of the Dana-Farber team.

title: GoFundMe for Boston officer charged in fatal shooting soars past $400K
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston officer Nicholas O'Malley charged with manslaughter, GoFundMe for his family exceeds $407,000.
Officer O'Malley suspended by Massachusetts police oversight board, currently on administrative leave with pay.
Incident involved fatal shooting of carjacking suspect Stephenson King during police confrontation in Roxbury.

title: AI attack ads are hitting Massachusetts political campaigns
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers consider new bills to regulate AI use in political ads, addressing potential voter misinformation.
Republican candidates Marc Lombardo and Brian Shortsleeve used AI-generated content in recent Massachusetts political campaigns.
Proposed bills H.5093 and H.5094 target deceptive AI media and require disclosure of synthetic content in political ads.
